claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0]) == n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of n^2 equals n^2.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n*n+1) if (n*n) % d == 0]) == n*n

claim: For all odd integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all odd divisors d of n equals (n+1)/2.
predicate: (n % 2 == 0) or (n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % 2 == 1]) == (n+1)//2)

claim: For all odd integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all even divisors d of n equals (n-1)/2.
predicate: (n % 2 == 0) or (n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % 2 == 0]) == (n-1)//2)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of 2n equals 2n.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, 2*n+1) if (2*n) % d == 0]) == 2*n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of n that are coprime to 2 equals phi(n).
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and gcd(d, 2) == 1]) == phi(n)

claim: For all prime p >= 2, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of p equals p.
predicate: not is_prime(n) or (n >= 2 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0]) == n)

claim: For all prime p >= 3, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of p^2 equals p^2.
predicate: not is_prime(n) or (n >= 3 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n*n+1) if (n*n) % d == 0]) == n*n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the number of divisors d of n for which phi(d) is odd equals the number of square divisors of n.
predicate: n >= 1 and len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and phi(d) % 2 == 1]) == len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and int(sqrt(d))**2 == d])

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all square divisors d of n equals tau(n).
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and int(sqrt(d))**2 == d]) == tau(n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the product of phi(d) over all divisors d of n is divisible by n.
predicate: n >= 1 and (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0]) == n) and (prod([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0]) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all divisors d of n that are prime equals the number of distinct prime factors of n.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and is_prime(d)]) == len(set([p for p in range(2, n+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is squarefree, then the sum of phi(d) over prime divisors d of n equals the number of prime factors of n.
predicate: n >= 1 and (not all([vp(n, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, n+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) or (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and is_prime(d)]) == len([p for p in range(2, n+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the average value of phi(d) over divisors d of n is at least n / (2 * tau(n)).
predicate: n >= 1 and (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0]) / len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0])) >= n / (2 * tau(n))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then the average of phi(d) over divisors of n is at least (n+1)/2.
predicate: not is_prime(n) or (n >= 2 and (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0]) / 2) >= (n+1)/2)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the largest phi(d) among divisors d of n is phi(n).
predicate: n >= 1 and max([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0]) == phi(n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the second-largest phi(d) among divisors d of n is at most phi(n/2) if n is even.
predicate: n >= 1 and (n % 2 == 1) or (max([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and phi(d) < phi(n)]) <= phi(n//2))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the number of divisors d of n with phi(d) = d-1 equals the number of prime divisors of n.
predicate: n >= 1 and len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and phi(d) == d-1]) == len([p for p in range(2, n+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a product of two distinct primes, then exactly two divisors d of n satisfy phi(d) = d-1.
predicate: n >= 1 and (len([p for p in range(2, n+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and phi(d) == d-1]) == 2)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over divisors d of n that are powers of 2 equals n if n is a power of 2, else 1.
predicate: n >= 1 and (n & (n-1) != 0) or (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d > 0 and (d & (d-1)) == 0]) == n)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over divisors d of n that are powers of an odd prime p equals the highest power of p dividing n.
predicate: n >= 1 and (not any([is_prime(p) and p % 2 == 1 and vp(n, p) > 0 for p in range(3, n+1)])) or all([sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % p == 0 and all([d % q != 0 for q in range(2, d) if q != p and is_prime(q)])]) == vp(n, p) for p in range(3, n+1) if is_prime(p) and p % 2 == 1 and vp(n, p) > 0])

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over divisors d of n that are multiples of 3 equals n/3 if 3 divides n, else 0.
predicate: n >= 1 and (n % 3 != 0) or (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % 3 == 0]) == n//3)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if 3 divides n, then the sum of phi(d) over divisors d of n not divisible by 3 equals (2n)/3.
predicate: n >= 1 and (n % 3 != 0) or (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % 3 != 0]) == (2*n)//3)

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over divisors d of n with d ≡ 1 mod 4 equals the number of solutions to x^2 ≡ -1 mod n.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % 4 == 1]) == len([x for x in range(1, n+1) if (x*x + 1) % n == 0])

claim: For all odd integers n >= 1, the number of divisors d of n with d ≡ 1 mod 4 equals the number of divisors d of n with d ≡ 3 mod 4, if n is a product of primes ≡ 1 mod 4.
predicate: n >= 1 and (n % 2 == 0) or (not all([p % 4 == 1 for p in range(2, n+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) or (len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % 4 == 1]) == len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d % 4 == 3]))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over divisors d of n that are perfect squares equals the number of square divisors of n.
predicate: n >= 1 and sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and int(sqrt(d))**2 == d]) == len([d for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and int(sqrt(d))**2 == d])

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is squarefree, then the sum of phi(d) over square divisors d of n equals tau(n).
predicate: n >= 1 and (not all([vp(n, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, n+1) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) or (sum([phi(d) for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and int(sqrt(d))**2 == d]) == tau(n))
